alerts.headline: Special Weather Statement issued March 3 at 7:38PM EST by NWS Boston/Norton MA
alerts.description:
Widespread rain and freezing rain will continue into the overnight
hours...but should come to an end toward daybreak.
However...residual moisture, light winds and the cold ground from
our recent weather will be favorable for the formation of black
ice. This may also be accompanied by some freezing fog too.
Motorists traveling early Wednesday morning should allow extra
time to reach their destination and be prepared for black ice.
Untreated walkways and parking lots may also become very slippery
for pedestrians. Temperatures should rise above freezing by mid-
late Wednesday morning alleviating any black ice issues.

alerts.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 3 at 1:02PM EST until March 4 at 7:00AM EST by NWS Boston/Norton MA
alerts.description:
* WHAT...Mixed precipitation. Total snow accumulations up to two
inches and ice accumulations around one-tenth of an inch.

* WHERE...Portions of northern Connecticut, central, eastern,
northeastern, and western Massachusetts, and northern Rhode Island.

* WHEN...Until 7 AM EST Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Power outages and tree damage are likely due to the ice.
Travel could be nearly impossible. The hazardous conditions could
impact the Tuesday evening and Wednesday morning commutes.

nws_forecast.name: Tonight
nws_forecast.detailed: Rain and areas of fog before midnight, then rain and areas of fog and a chance of freezing rain. Cloudy, with a low around 31. Northwest wind around 6 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. Little or no ice accumulation expected.

nws_forecast.detailed: Areas of fog before 8am. Mostly sunny, with a high near 46. Southwest wind 3 to 8 mph. Little or no ice accumulation expected.

nws_forecast.detailed: Areas of fog after 11pm. Partly cloudy, with a low around 29. Southwest wind around 5 mph.

nws_forecast.detailed: Areas of fog before 8am, then a chance of rain between 10am and 2pm, then a chance of sleet and a chance of rain between 2pm and 3pm, then areas of fog and a chance of sleet and a chance of rain. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 41. East wind 2 to 6 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
